run_summary: Portfolio is 6 open positions, +$134 cumulative unrealized (balanced), with 4 showing positive returns. Track record shows 50% hit rate overall but sharp divergence: OVERDONE at 60%, UNDERDONE at 0%—this is the key calibration signal. New flags include DECK (re-flag, already long), ALK (OVERDONE conf 3 on oil/macro), and BLDR (already long in two tranches, now re-flagged UNDERDONE on housing bill). Management is to hold core positions and be extremely disciplined on re-entries and low-conviction UNDERDONE calls given empirical track record.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Q4/FY earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
entries.thesis: DECK filed an earnings 8-K (item 2.02) and is moving up sharply on 1.6x volume. The stock has been deeply derated over the past year (trading near $100 from prior highs well above), so a beat-and-raise into a beaten-down setup typically gets follow-through over days as analysts revise. A 5-day cumulative move that's only modestly above today's print suggests the market hasn't yet fully chewed through the implications; sentiment remains bearish on UGG/HOKA brand fatigue, and a clean print can drive multiple expansion off depressed levels.
entries.what_kills: Guidance was actually maintained/lowered and the pop is short-covering only; HOKA growth decelerated further; inventory build flagged in the release; tariff exposure (Vietnam/China sourcing) called out as worsening
entries.reasoning: This is a re-flag of an existing long position (6 shares, already +8.28%). No new information beyond the same Q4/FY earnings catalyst that justified the original entry 3 days ago. Position is already established and performing well; additional shares are not needed. Analyst upgrade cycle is playing out as flagged thesis predicted. Watch for follow-through over next 2-3 days but do not add; thesis is intact in existing position.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group
entries.catalyst: Oil below $100 on Trump Iran 'final stages' comments; fuel-cost relief narrative
entries.thesis: ALK's outsized move appears driven entirely by the Iran-talks/oil-down narrative rather than any company-specific catalyst — the only 8-K is a 5.07 annual meeting vote, which is non-event. Move magnitude exceeds SKYW (peer) and the broader IWM by a wide margin on volume only 1.2x average, suggesting short-cover/momentum chasing into a single macro headline that can reverse if Iran talks stall. Fuel tailwind is real but lower-quality balance sheet airlines often overshoot peers on these reflex bids.
entries.what_kills: Confirmed Iran framework agreement with sustained oil decline; ALK-specific positive guidance or route news; sector-wide upgrade cycle from sell-side
entries.reasoning: Flagged as OVERDONE conf 3 on oil/Iran de-escalation macro narrative. My own track record shows OVERDONE at 60% hit rate, which is solid. However, this flag has critical weaknesses: (1) no company-specific catalyst URL provided—the flag cites only macro ('Trump Iran final stages', oil price), making it pure sentiment fade rather than catalyst-driven; (2) confidence is 3, which against OVERDONE calls historically sits at 60% and should be a core conviction tier, but the analysis itself reads as relative-value (ALK overshooting vs. SKYW) rather than absolute; (3) 'what_kills' includes 'confirmed Iran framework agreement'—if that happens, the macro headwind reverses entirely, and fade positions are forced to cover into the turn. The thesis is coherent, but without a named company catalyst and with macro-only conviction, the risk/reward is poor. Fade airline sympathy spikes are typically shorter-duration than earnings-driven plays. SKIP.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: House passes housing affordability bill (CNBC: 'win for investors'); 8-K with 5.02 director/officer changes around annual meeting
entries.thesis: House approved housing affordability bill today (removed industry-opposed build-to-rent requirements — explicitly framed as 'win for investors'). BLDR is a direct beneficiary as the largest building products supplier. Move is on only 1.02x volume despite a meaningful policy catalyst, and the stock is still negative on a 5-day basis — suggesting the housing-bill news hasn't been fully absorbed by funds yet. The 8-K's 5.02 officer item adds some noise but appears coincident with annual meeting (5.07 alongside), likely board-rotation routine.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 item turns out to be unplanned CFO/CEO departure; Senate kills bill; housing data prints weak
entries.reasoning: This is a re-flag of an already-long position (currently holding 17 shares across two tranches, average cost ~$71.45, current +3.72% average). The new flag cites the House housing bill as a fresh catalyst alongside the 5.02 officer change. However, I already hold BLDR on the exact same housing-bill logic (second tranche entered just 2 days ago). New information would be: (1) Senate passage confirmed, or (2) the 5.02 officer item resolved as routine (annual meeting context supports routine). Neither has materialized. The flag's confidence is 3, and my UNDERDONE track record is 0% (n=1)—very weak. Adding to a position I already flagged as housing-bill beneficiary 2 days ago risks chasing my own thesis instead of waiting for extrinsic confirmation (Senate action, analyst upgrades). WATCH pending Senate movement or 8-K clarification on the officer item. No additional buy warranted at this time.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
entries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
entries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
entries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
entries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector strength in defense names (ITA +0.99%); AVAV is high-beta within that group. No company-specific catalyst visible.
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with ITA +0.99% and ongoing defense procurement narrative (drones/loitering munitions remain a priority regardless of any Iran de-escalation — Ukraine/Pacific demand persists). Magnitude is larger than the sector ETF but AVAV is high-beta within defense and is a pure-play on the drone theme that has been a structural winner. No 8-K, no manufactured thesis warranted — the move looks proportionate to ongoing tailwinds rather than mispriced.
entries.what_kills: An Iran peace deal genuinely reduces defense spending expectations and AVAV gives back gains — would suggest the move was actually overdone and tied to the Iran narrative inverse to what I'm reading.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
entries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Unidentified; SMH +3.8% tape provides partial explanation but not full magnitude
entries.thesis: LSCC up sharply on 1.4x volume with empty catalyst_signals. SMH +3.8% explains some of it but LSCC tripled that. Possible drivers: sell-side note, M&A whisper, or AI-FPGA narrative tailwind — but I cannot identify which from available evidence. Neither bull nor bear case clears conf 3.

entries.name: Onto Innovation
entries.catalyst: SMH +3.81% sector lift + mean reversion from prior week weakness
entries.thesis: ONTO was down 5% over the prior five sessions and is now recovering on a strong semi-equipment tape. The 8-K items (5.07 vote results, 8.01 other) are routine. Magnitude is in line with semicap peers given SMH +3.8%. No mispricing thesis stronger than 'mean reversion + sector beta' is articulable.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Defensive sector rotation + technical bounce; 5.07 filing is non-substantive
entries.thesis: RGEN's 8-K is item 5.07 (typically annual meeting voting results), which is administrative and not a catalyst. The move is consistent with XLV +1.1% defensive rotation and a bounce off a recent multi-day decline. No clear mispricing — magnitude is appropriate given sector rotation and recent weakness.
entries.what_kills: 8-K exhibit contains something other than vote results (unlikely for 5.07-only)

entries.name: SkyWest, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; likely sympathy with oil-price-driven airline weakness
entries.thesis: SKYW has continued lower over multiple sessions with no 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings, and on below-average volume. Most likely driver is jet fuel pressure from XLE/oil bid on Iran tensions and broader risk-off cyclical selling, but with no idiosyncratic signal I can't make a directional call at conf 3+.
